_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of transformer architecture, attention mechanisms, and the pre-training and fine-tuning paradigm as it applies to language models.
comments: DCWF ksa superset of WIDAI AI domain. AI-K-012 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AI-S-011 (AI, cosine: 0.541), AI-K-011 (AI, cosine: 0.500).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.4/10 (raw 0.439). Shared vocabulary: [knowledge, language]. DCWF-only terms [s, target] vs WIDAI-only terms [applies, architecture, attention, fine, mechanisms, models] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.863 (P=0.876, R=0.850). P exceeds R by 0.026 — the DCWF element's 4 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 18 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). "Knowledge of target language(s)." and "Knowledge of transformer architecture, attention mechanisms, and the pre-trainin" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.567 > STS 0.439 (gap +0.128). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [knowledge, language] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 14.3%. Shared tokens: [knowledge, language]. DCWF-only: [s, target]. WIDAI-only: [applies, architecture, attention, fine, mechanisms, models].
